raw_consumer_statement:
I purchased an open box TV from Best Buy on Saturday, XX/XX/XXXX on the Best Buy credit card. It was a {$460.00} discount from a sale price on the sale price of the TV, {$1900.00}. The open box discount is a line item on the receipt that comes off as a discount from whatever the TV is selling for at that time. Today ( XX/XX/XXXX ) I looked and the cost of the TV dropped to {$1700.00} on sale and theyre now selling open box for {$1200.00}. All of this is on Best Buys website, Im not asking for a price match. I called them and I asked them if they would honor THEIR price drop since its only been 3 days. They told me on the phone they cant do that and to go to the store. So I drive the 30 minutes to the store and the manager blows me off and has the associate tell me he wont do anything. I then had to request he come over and speak to me. He came over and told me he cant do anything because, our systems wont let us, which is frankly absurd. I asked if he could meet me in the middle, he refused. I asked if he could try and throw in a warranty to make up the difference, he refused. His only solution was to, bring the TV back and return it. Then we can scan it and see if it costs less and you can buy it again. 

Its an XXXX TV that weighs XXXX pounds, you can not be serious with this customer service.
